title: Proof Boundary — Executive Value Before the Resume
supported_claims: A resume is a compressed artifact that cannot fully demonstrate how an executive frames problems or makes decisions.
Public writing and frameworks can provide context before formal application review.
A coherent body of work can make an executive operating philosophy more legible to recruiters and opportunity sponsors.
unsupported_claims: Any universal claim that public publishing improves hiring outcomes.
Any quantified conversion claim for recruiters, interviews, or offers.
Any claim that resumes are unnecessary or ineffective.
